name: Bezalel
shortDescription: Bezalel was the chief artisan commissioned by God to construct the Tabernacle and its sacred objects, including the Ark of the Covenant and the Menorah. He is revered for his divinely inspired skill and craftsmanship.
description: Bezalel ben Uri ben Hur, of the tribe of Judah, is a central figure in the Book of Exodus, renowned as the master craftsman chosen by God to oversee the construction of the Tabernacle, the Ark of the Covenant, and all its furnishings. His appointment is detailed in Exodus 31:1-11, where God states that He has filled Bezalel with the Spirit of God, endowing him with wisdom, understanding, knowledge, and all kinds of craftsmanship. This divine inspiration enabled Bezalel to design and create intricate works in gold, silver, bronze, stone, and wood, surpassing ordinary human skill. He worked alongside Aholiab, from the tribe of Dan, who assisted him with weaving and embroidery. Bezalel's most significant achievements include the Ark of the Covenant, the Menorah, the altar of incense, the altar of burnt offering, and the priestly garments. These items were not merely functional objects, but sacred symbols of God's presence and covenant with the Israelites. Bezalel's craftsmanship was crucial in establishing a tangible representation of the Shekhinah, the divine presence, within the Tabernacle. His legacy extends beyond his specific creations; he represents the ideal of divinely inspired artistry and the importance of skilled labor in religious practice. His story highlights the belief that artistic talent can be a gift from God, intended to be used for sacred purposes.
quests: Construction of the Ark of the Covenant
Supervising the construction of the Tabernacle

sources.title: The Jewish Study Bible
sources.author: Berlin, Adele, and Brettler, Marc Zvi
